channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Remake Leak Confirms New Content
item.content:encoded:
A new leak has seemingly provided confirmation that the upcoming Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced title will feature new content.

The Indonesian Game Ratings System (IGRS) has just suffered a massive security breach, resulting in details about several games leaking online. Over 1 hour of spoiler-filled footage of 007 First Light has already leaked online, and Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced was a part of the leak. Now, the summary has indicated that Black Flag Remake will receive new content.

Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Rating Leak Confirms New Characters and Stories

Spotted by PedroJosLagesM1 (via TheRealZephryss) on X (formerly Twitter), the rating of Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced has appeared on IGRS‘ website. We already saw the PEGI rating late last year, which indicated the game would be PEGI 18, and it has received an 18+ on IGRS. The new information is the game’s synopsis, which confirms the new details.

The summary highlights that the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced “breathes new life” into the experience while staying “faithful to the spirit of the original.” The final line confirms the new content, stating, “Modernized in 2026, the new game features a cast of new characters and stories.” You can find the full description below.

This title breathes new life into a fan favourite while remaining carefully faithful to the spirit of the original. It follows the story of charismatic pirate and Assassin Edward Kenway as he adventures through 18th Century Caribbean with parkours in cities, and battles at seas, in search of fortune and glory. Modernized in 2026, the new game includes a cast of new characters and stories.

In other news, despite all the rumors and leaks, Ubisoft confirmed Assassin’s Creed Black Flag remake is real and will be called ‘Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced’ just last month. What are your thoughts on new content being confirmed for Assassin’s Creed Black Flag remake? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Metro 2039 being officially confirmed and a reveal coming next week. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Backyard Baseball’s Comeback Isn’t Just About Nostalgia
item.content:encoded:
Bringing Backyard Baseball back was always going to come with one big question. Would it just feel like the old games, or would it actually hold up as a modern sports title?

The answer, at least from the team behind it, is somewhere in the middle.

“I feel like the reality is every game needs a pick up and play first time user experience,” said Mega Cat Studios CEO James Deighan. “So we have that, and that’s been a major effort to kind of optimize and track against and make sure we’re delivering against this promise that it’s accessible to everybody.”

That accessibility has always been at the core of Backyard Sports. But this time around, it’s being paired with systems that go far beyond what the original games were capable of.

Arcade First, But Not Shallow

From the start, the team at Playground Productions and Mega Cat Studios made a conscious decision about where Backyard Baseball should sit on the sports game spectrum.

“I think this lives closer to the arcade side,” Deighan said. “Backyard sports with the characters and the world, so much of it kind of skews towards that arcade experience too, because you’re suspending reality for these humorous little league moments.”

  • More Backyard Baseball: From Classroom to Comeback: How Backyard Sports Was Brought Back to Life

That means faster gameplay, more exaggerated action, and a bigger focus on personality and feedback.

“So much of what keeps you engaged is these characters are always active and moving and doing things,” he said. “So I think the gameplay has to also be a little bit snappier, a little bit more, we’ll say engaging, at least from like the visual and audio feedback side.”

But just because it leans arcade doesn’t mean it’s staying simple.

“You can’t have a great sports game without having great stats progression,” Deighan said. “And I feel like some of the rewards players get now is we have advanced stats progression, and we have some other things we have not yet announced yet that I think is really going to be in the same vein of exciting to the legacy fans that want something they can come back and play for 10 years.”

A Different Kind Of Difficulty

One of the more interesting changes comes in how the game handles difficulty. Instead of just making things faster or more precise, Backyard Baseball leans into what made the original games feel unique: chaos.

“I think backyard versus other sports games, our version of hard is that means that kids are going to fall over more and there’s going to be different parts [of that],” said Playground Productions CEO Lindsay Barnett.

That idea shows up clearly in modes like Backyard Derby, which players have already gotten hands-on time with.

“If you want to play crazy pitch, then you’re going to be trying to hit a bunch of power-up pitches that Mr. Clanky, the robot, is going to be throwing you,” Barnett said. “If you want to play an easier version, play the classic one where you get classic pitches.”

And those crazy pitches go well beyond anything grounded in reality.

“In the crazy pitch mode, you’re going to have to hit fireballs and freeze pitches and zigzags and spitballs,” she said.

It’s all designed to reflect the unpredictability of kids playing sports.

“If anybody has kids and you see what it looks like when they’re playing actual little league, it’s the unexpected that makes it so hard and funny,” Barnett said. “You get kids who are picking daisies in the outfield and doing somersaults in the baseline.”

Built For Replayability

While the original Backyard Baseball games were limited by their time constraints, the new version is being built with replayability in mind from the ground up.

That starts with competition.

“We have a global leaderboard and even our internal team, we know everybody’s usernames, and we’re getting very competitive to try to have the top spots,” Barnett said. “I want to be the best backyard player in the world.”

But it doesn’t stop there.

“I think achievements in this game, you’re going to see us roll out some pretty exciting ways of having achievements that are going to keep people coming back for more, and things like unlockables, and there’s a lot of good stuff,” she added.

Behind the scenes, that replayability is being carefully structured.

“When we were coming up with the reward schedule and the retention plan of how to make sure that the players feel sticky and excited and want to keep playing,” Deighan said, “we were making sure that there’s a balance between these perishable rewards that are only consumable in a session and only happen under these certain events, as well as unlockable rewards and content.”

The goal is to give players reasons to keep coming back, whether that’s daily, weekly, or just whenever they feel like jumping in. And unlike most modern sports franchises, Backyard Baseball isn’t being built as an annual release.

Instead, the plan is to grow it over time.

“This is Backyard Baseball this is not Backyard Baseball 2026,” Barnett said, adding that they aren’t designing a game that will have fans buy a new Backyard Baseball every year. “This is a game that we can iterate on, we can listen to fans and hear what they want.”

That approach opens the door for new modes, characters, updates, and additions without resetting everything every year.

“We get to have the ability to incorporate that into this Backyard Baseball,” she said.

It’s a different way of thinking about a sports game, especially one rooted in nostalgia. Because while Backyard Baseball is absolutely leaning into what made it special in the first place, it’s also trying to build something that lasts. Something that players don’t just return to once, but keep coming back to over time.

And if the Barnett, Deighan, and the rest of the team behind it gets its way, it won’t just feel like the games people remember. It’ll become a new favorite all over again.

What are your thoughts on the return of Backyard Baseball? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

This story is part of a deeper interview with Playground Productions CEO Lindsay Barnett and Mega Cat Studios founder and CEO James Deighan. The full interview with the pair will be released later in April.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Stone Cold Steve Austin Comes to Fortnite This Week
item.content:encoded:
Get ready to crack open a cold Steveweiser, drink a swig of beer for the working man, and unleash a can of whoop ass in Fortnite with Stone Cold Steve Austin starting on April 16.

Fortnite has confirmed that a Steve Austin skin will be joining the game this Thursday. WWE WrestleMania takes place this weekend, and Austin’s inclusion in the popular Battle Royale is all part of the celebration for the biggest WWE event of the year.

Will Liv Morgan Join Stone Cold in Fortnite?

When it was first rumored that Steve Austin was coming to the game, it was also stated that Liv Morgan, a current WWE Superstar and the challenger for the WWE Women’s World Championship this weekend, would also be joining the game. She has not been confirmed yet.

Morgan, by virtue of winning the WWE Royal Rumble in January, earned the right to challenge Stephanie Vaquer at WrestleMania. That match will take place on the first night of WrestleMania, Saturday, April 18.

Stone Cold Steve Austin’s Greatest Rival Is Part of Fortnite Lore

In the days of the WWE Attitude Era, Austin was best known for his feud with The Rock. Dwayne Johnson is in Fortnite, but he’s part of the deeper game lore as he portrays The Foundation. Other WWE Superstars featured in the game as skins include The Undertaker, John Cena, and Bianca Belair. Cena will also be part of this weekend’s WrestleMania, hosting the event.

Steve Austin made his video game debut in WCW Main Event on the Nintendo Game Boy in 1994. He has been in nearly every WWE game since WWF War Zone in 1998. Now, he becomes even more of a legendary video game character by joining the Battle Royale which may put him as an ally or rival to Marvel characters such as Spider-Man, legendary cartoon characters like Bugs Bunny or SpongeBob SquarePants, and other crazy crossovers.

If you’re ready to kick some ass with Stone Cold Steve Austin in Fortnite, leave a “Hell yeah!” in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: When Is GTA 6 Coming Out On PC? GTA 6 PC Release, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Grand Theft Auto 6 is releasing on most major platforms, and while a PS5 and Xbox Series X|S day-one launch is certain, there’s a bit of the unknown hanging over the PC version. With GTA 6 expected to sell like hotcakes everywhere, let’s check out the latest on the GTA 6 PC port and when it will release.

Rockstar Games must be feeling the weight of the world on its shoulders about GTA 6. It’s the biggest game ever. Period. No question. The delays are painful, but it means the final product will be out of this world. It’s unclear how smoothly GTA 6 will run on PS5 and Xbox Series X|S, but those versions are happening, as is a PC presence.

GTA 6 is going to be a beast on PC, so let’s find out when it’s happening.

Is GTA 6 Coming Out On PC At Launch?

Take-Two Interactive has confirmed that GTA 6 will not be on PC at launch, as the game will ‘start with a console or two before branching out.’

If you look back at GTA 5, this shouldn’t be a surprise. Grand Theft Auto 5 eventually came out on PC nearly two years after the original PS3 and Xbox 360 debut!

  • More Insider Gaming: Will GTA 6 Be Delayed Again in 2026?

Sensibly, this was done to optimize GTA 5 and ensure it was ready for a PC port. With GTA 6’s size likely to dwarf GTA 5, there’s even more emphasis on making sure GTA 6 is good to go for a smooth PC experience.

To quote Insider Gaming’s own article on Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick talking GTA 6 not coming to PC at launch:

“When you have a big title in the market and we have many of them coming, historically that has sold consoles,” Zelnick said while also mentioning that it’s not always the case to not release on all platforms at once. He pointed to Civilization 7 as an example of a full multi-platform release that includes PC and Nintendo Switch.”

So, a PC port is happening, just not right away.

When Will GTA 6 Be On PC?

While there is nothing official on this, it’s expected that GTA 6 will not release on PC until later into 2027, possibly even into 2028, based on previous evidence.

Rockstar has refused to commit to a PC release date yet, but this is understandable given the console release still has an aura of uncertainty until it actually happens—another delay could be the latest annoying spanner in the works.

  • More Insider Gaming: GTA 6 Hackers Say Stolen Data Will Be Published

Corsair’s CEO believed GTA 6 was coming to PC in early 2026, but we know delays have altered all plans. If the November 19 release goes ahead, and Rockstar follows a similar pattern to GTA 5 (arriving roughly 17 months later), then it could be as late as April 2028 for a GTA 6 PC port.

Are you going to wait for a full PC release before buying GTA 6? Or is it a day one, must-buy, must-have purchase you can start playing right away on PlayStation and Xbox? Let us know what you’re going to do via our free-to-join Insider Gaming Discord.

A new GTA 6 ‘leak’ containing alleged footage forgotten for years is more hot GTA 6 content you should check out.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: ComeBack to Stand-In for NAVI in VCT EMEA, it’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
Berkcan ‘ComeBack’ Şentürk is set to join NAVI’s Valorant roster as a stand-in, according to a report by Sheep Esports. The Duelist will reportedly take the place of Abdullah ‘ExiT’ Al-Twaijri, who cannot travel due to the disruption caused by the 2026 Iran War.

As a result, Herman ‘Kolosha’ Skrypka will return to the NAVI Junior academy roster, who stood in for ExiT in the opening two matches of VCT EMEA Stage 1.

ComeBack to stand-in for NAVI after abrupt Team Heretics benching

ComeBack was suddenly benched following a 2-0 loss, ironically to NAVI, in Team Heretic’s opening VCT EMEA Stage 1 match.

Team Heretics made a video explaining the decision, with their coach, Neil ‘neilzinho’ Finlay, explaining that they had a “fundamental disagreement after the NAVI game in how we see the game as a team.” The Heretics coach added, “He does not fit in this roster.”

ComeBack agreed with the assessment, stating that his playstyle and approach were “completely different” (translated) from the rest of the team.

The 18-year-old will now reportedly join NAVI as a stand-in after only one competitive match day away from the server. NAVI will next play Gentle Mates on April 15 in VCT EMEA Stage 1.

Is NAVI the right fit for ComeBack?

After a mismatch with Heretics, the question of whether his playstyle can match that of NAVI will be hanging over the heads of both the player and the organization.

ComeBack’s playstyle bias seemed more towards solo, explosive individual plays. While that’s natural for a Duelist, NAVI generally tend towards a more considered and slow style, a hallmark of Kyrylo ‘ANGE1’ Karasov teams.

Still, NAVI will still have the Turkish connection in the roster with Uğur ‘Ruxic’ Güç, Emirhan ‘hiro’ Kat, and Berkcan ”ComeBack’ Şentürk in the roster, the latter two being dual nationals of Turkey.

Combined with hiro and Ruxic’s almost telepathic connection, perhaps ComeBack can make use of that synergy, especially in the clutch when they are the only players alive and free to speak in the language.

Despite being announced on April 1, NAVI are yet to play with the full five of their new roster due to ExiT’s absence. The roster has been playing with NAVI Junior’s Kolosha.

The Ukrainian academy player had a tough debut in the team’s victory against Heretics, showing an improved face despite the loss to FUT in the second match of the split.

Yet that improvement was not enough, as the organization has clearly chosen to have more experience in the form of ComeBack despite potential issues.

Would you prefer NAVI had stuck with Kolosha? Discuss that on our Discord server!
For more Valorant news, see the details of the VCT 2027 revamp.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson
